proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, plausibly age) are associated with longer pfs_months in the cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson correlation of feature_078 with pfs_months r=0.766, p=0. Strong positive linear relationship: each 10-unit increase in feature_078 maps to roughly 1.7 additional months of PFS in a simple linear fit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race categories recorded in feature_064 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_064 race categories: F=0.872, p=0.479. Group means span only 0.134 months ({'asian': 4.305735282321731, 'black': 4.273362668088063, 'hispanic': 4.314635687573159, 'other': 4.407088298549452, 'white': 4.315667554390913}); no clinically meaningful difference detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories in feature_018 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_018 insurance categories: F=1.475, p=0.219. Group means span 0.082 months ({'medicaid': 4.276909402532673, 'medicare': 4.311569491987206, 'private': 4.330219858522746, 'uninsured': 4.248637881550722}); no significant disparity in raw PFS by insurance type.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0 (negative effect of feature_051).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: pfs_months mean 3.703 (n=27481, feature_051=1) vs 5.055 (n=22519, feature_051=0). Difference = -1.352 months, p=0. Strongly negative: feature_051=1 confers ~1.35 months shorter PFS, the largest binary main effect in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (positive main effect of feature_038, suggesting a beneficial intervention or favorable feature).

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: pfs_months mean 5.089 (n=10022, feature_038=1) vs 4.117 (n=39978, feature_038=0). Difference = +0.972 months, p=1.71e-216. feature_038=1 is associated with ~0.97 months longer PFS overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature_057 (values 0-2) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone manner.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.360, p=0. Monotone-decreasing PFS with each increment of feature_057. Group means: {0: 5.271274530331505, 1: 4.053644171440389, 2: 2.9077880804279204}. In an OLS frame each unit higher feature_057 ≈ -1.17 months of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_013 = 0.
Patients with feature_043 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_043 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 mean 4.121 (n=20898) vs feature_013=0 mean 4.448 (n=29102); diff=-0.327 months, p=1.86e-58.
feature_043=1 mean 4.114 (n=17657) vs feature_043=0 mean 4.419 (n=32343); diff=-0.305 months, p=1.04e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous laboratory-like feature_092 (range ~1.5-5.5) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.102, p=3.25e-115. Modest but very robust positive correlation; each one-unit increase in feature_092 ≈ +0.47 months PFS in OLS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, range ~0-25) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.123, p=2.21e-167. Higher feature_099 modestly but very significantly tracks worse PFS; OLS slope ≈ -0.077 months per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_009 (continuous, heavily right-skewed) are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.0377, p=3.13e-17. Weak but statistically significant negative correlation. Effect is small relative to feature_092 / feature_099, suggesting feature_009 is a minor adverse marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the rare binary marker feature_109 = 1 (~5% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_109=1 mean 4.094 (n=2272) vs feature_109=0 mean 4.322 (n=47728); diff=-0.228 months, p=3.47e-07. Modest but reproducible adverse effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the rare binary marker feature_067 = 1 (~3% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0.

The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is restricted to patients with feature_013 = 0 (i.e., feature_013 = 1 abrogates the feature_038 effect). Equivalently, the feature_038 x feature_013 interaction term is negative.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_038*feature_013; plus stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.629 months, p=7.69e-226. Stratified: feature_013=0 -> feature_038 adds 1.656 months (p=6.07e-293); feature_013=1 -> feature_038 adds 0.027 months (p=0.456). feature_013 = 1 essentially eliminates the feature_038 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is also restricted to patients with feature_043 = 0; feature_043 = 1 abolishes the effect (negative feature_038 x feature_043 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.454 months, p=4.08e-168. Stratified: feature_043=0 -> feature_038 adds 1.480 months (p=3.72e-274); feature_043=1 -> 0.026 months (p=0.516). Same pattern as feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 is further reduced or eliminated in patients with feature_109 = 1 (negative feature_038 x feature_109 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.183 months, p=6.13e-23. feature_109=0 -> +1.027 mo (p=5.2e-226); feature_109=1 -> -0.156 mo (p=0.134). feature_109 = 1 drives the feature_038 effect to slightly negative / null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are simultaneously feature_013 = 0, feature_043 = 0, and feature_109 = 0 ("triple-negative" biomarker subgroup) experience a large pfs_months benefit from feature_038, while patients positive for any of those three markers do not.

analyses.code: Define wt = (f013==0) & (f043==0) & (f109==0); compare feature_038 effect within wt vs not
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-negative subgroup (n=17372): feature_038=1 mean 6.875 (n=3461) vs feature_038=0 mean 4.117 (n=13911); diff=+2.758 months, p=0. Within marker-positive subgroup (n=32628): diff=0.030 months, p=0.294. The clinical takeaway: feature_038 is a highly effective intervention but only in triple-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 = 1 enhances rather than diminishes the feature_038 benefit (positive feature_038 x feature_067 interaction); after accounting for the wild-type composite biomarker the effect may attenuate.
After conditioning on the triple-negative wild-type composite, feature_067 no longer modifies the feature_038 effect.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.988 months, p=1.2e-11. Stratified: feature_067=0 -> feature_038 adds 0.941 mo (p=2.02e-199); feature_067=1 -> feature_038 adds 1.929 mo (p=2.83e-21).

analyses.code: Within wt subgroup, compare feature_038 effect by feature_067
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-negative wild-type subgroup, feature_067=0: feature_038 effect = +2.749 months; feature_067=1: +2.906 months. After conditioning on wild-type, feature_067 no longer materially modifies the feature_038 effect; the apparent univariate enhancement was confounded by feature_067 being more often co-occurring with the triple-negative state.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is amplified at older values of feature_078 (negative feature_051 x feature_078 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0071 per unit feature_078, p=1.9e-09. The slope of feature_078 is shallower among feature_051=1 patients, consistent with feature_051 attenuating the apparent positive feature_078-PFS relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051 differs across the ordinal feature_057 levels (modification of the feature_051 effect by feature_057).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_051 + feature_057 + feature_051*feature_057; plus stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = 0.063, p=0.0191. Stratified by feature_057 the feature_051 deficit is fairly constant (f057=0 -> -1.40 mo, f057=1 -> -1.35 mo, f057=2 -> -1.27 mo); the interaction is statistically detectable but small in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months effects of feature_051 and feature_038 are independent; their interaction term is not significant after accounting for both main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = 0.070, p=0.151. Both main effects remain large and significant (feature_051 ≈ -1.37, feature_038 ≈ +0.94 in this two-way model); the additional interaction term is small and non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative wild-type biomarker subgroup, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit does not differ meaningfully across race strata in feature_064.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_038 effect by feature_064 within wt subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 effect (months PFS) within wt subgroup by race: hispanic: +3.21 mo, asian: +2.88 mo, white: +2.63 mo, other: +3.07 mo, black: +2.79 mo. Effects span ~2.6-3.3 months across all five race categories with each individually highly significant (p < 1e-10). No evidence of disparate treatment benefit across race once confounding by biomarker prevalence is removed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative wild-type biomarker subgroup, the feature_038 PFS benefit is similar across insurance categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) in feature_018.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_038 effect by feature_018 within wt subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 effect within wt subgroup by insurance: private: +2.60 mo, medicare: +2.80 mo, uninsured: +2.75 mo, medicaid: +3.09 mo. All four insurance categories show a 2.6-3.1 month PFS benefit with p < 1e-15; no disparity detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous biomarker feature_092 modifies the feature_038 PFS effect (e.g. patients with higher feature_092 derive a different magnitude of benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.077 per unit feature_092, p=0.125. Both main effects are highly significant (feature_092 b=0.486, p=7.2e-104; feature_038 b=1.269, p=4.19e-11), but the interaction is not — feature_092 exerts an additive effect rather than modifying treatment efficacy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A combined OLS model containing the main effects of feature_051, feature_078, feature_057, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067, feature_092, feature_099 plus interactions feature_038*feature_013, feature_038*feature_043, feature_038*feature_109, and feature_051*feature_078 explains pfs_months substantially better than any single feature, with the three feature_038 x biomarker interactions remaining highly significant after adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_067 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043 + feature_038*feature_109 + feature_051*feature_078
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable model (R^2=0.896). Coefficients: feature_051=-0.918 (p=1.2e-96); feature_078=+0.180 (p=0); feature_057=-1.166 (p=0); feature_038=+2.289 (p=0); feature_013=-0.000 (p=0.98); feature_043=+0.003 (p=0.68); feature_109=+0.004 (p=0.81); feature_067=+0.011 (p=0.57); feature_092=+0.473 (p=0); feature_099=-0.077 (p=0); inter_38_13=-1.786 (p=0); inter_38_43=-1.471 (p=0); inter_38_109=-1.770 (p=0); inter_51_age=-0.007 (p=8.8e-24). The three feature_038 x biomarker interactions all retain p<1e-20 and the main effect of feature_038 (in the absence of those biomarkers) is +2.29 months PFS. Each binary biomarker individually drops the feature_038 benefit by ~1.5-1.8 months. feature_013/043/109 main effects shrink to ≈0 once interactions are present, indicating their association with PFS is exclusively via dampening the feature_038 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative wild-type subgroup (feature_013=0 & feature_043=0 & feature_109=0), the feature_038 benefit is at least as large in older (feature_078 above median) as in younger patients, indicating no need to age-restrict treatment.

analyses.code: Within wt, split by median feature_078 and compare feature_038 effect
analyses.result_summary: Below-median feature_078 (n=8669): feature_038 effect = +2.729 months (p=1.24e-244). At-or-above median (n=8703): feature_038 effect = +2.789 months (p=7.55e-236). Both subgroups show clinically meaningful, statistically significant benefit; feature_078 does not gate eligibility for the feature_038 benefit.
